mission.title: Install and Configure LiteLLM
mission.description: LiteLLM is a Python SDK and proxy server that allows you to call over 100 LLM APIs in a unified OpenAI format. It provides features such as cost tracking, load balancing, and logging, making it suitable for applications that require multiple LLM integrations. Key trade-offs include the complexity of setup and the need for proper resource allocation to handle multiple API calls efficiently.

steps.title: Install the CLI
steps.description:
Install the necessary command-line tools to interact with your Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

pip install litellm

```

> Install the LiteLLM Python SDK.

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
Create a new Kubernetes cluster with the specified version and node count.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create litellm-cluster --cluster-version=1.30 --num-nodes=3 --region=us-central1

```

> Ensure you have gcloud CLI installed and authenticated.

steps.title: Set Up Kubeconfig
steps.description:
Configure your local kubeconfig to access the newly created cluster.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials litellm-cluster --region=us-central1

```

> This command updates your kubeconfig with the new cluster credentials.

steps.title: Verify Cluster Nodes
steps.description:
Check that the nodes in your cluster are up and running.

```bash

kubectl get nodes

```

> You should see the nodes listed with a status of 'Ready'.

steps.title: Deploy LiteLLM
steps.description:
Deploy the LiteLLM application to your Kubernetes cluster.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/BerriAI/litellm/v1.82.dev2/deploy/k8s/litellm-deployment.yaml

```

> This command deploys LiteLLM using the provided Kubernetes manifest.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
Set up autoscaling and monitoring for your LiteLLM deployment.

```bash

kubectl autoscale deployment litellm --cpu-percent=50 --min=1 --max=10

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/BerriAI/litellm/v1.82.dev2/deploy/k8s/litellm-monitoring.yaml

```

> This command enables horizontal pod autoscaling.
> Deploy monitoring resources.

uninstall.title: Remove LiteLLM Deployment
uninstall.description:
This will remove the LiteLLM deployment and associated resources. Be aware that any data stored in the application may be lost.

```bash

kubectl delete -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/BerriAI/litellm/v1.82.dev2/deploy/k8s/litellm-deployment.yaml

gcloud container clusters delete litellm-cluster --region=us-central1 --quiet

```

> This command deletes the entire cluster and all associated resources.

upgrade.title: Upgrade LiteLLM Deployment
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up any important data. Then, perform the upgrade and verify the deployment.

```bash

kubectl get pods -l app=litellm -o jsonpath='{.items[*].metadata.name}' | xargs kubectl drain --ignore-daemonsets

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/BerriAI/litellm/v1.82.dev2/deploy/k8s/litellm-deployment.yaml

kubectl get pods -l app=litellm

```

> Cordon and drain the pods before upgrade.
> This command applies the updated deployment.
> Verify that the new pods are running.
commands: kubectl get pods -l app=litellm -o jsonpath='{.items[*].metadata.name}' | xargs kubectl drain --ignore-daemonsets

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to pull image'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified container image may not exist or is private.

**Fix:**
Check the image name and ensure it is accessible.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'CrashLoopBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The application may be misconfigured or missing environment variables.

**Fix:**
Review the deployment configuration and ensure all required environment variables are set.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient CPU resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The cluster does not have enough resources to schedule the pods.

**Fix:**
Increase the node size or add more nodes to the cluster.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Connection refused'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The LiteLLM service may not be running or is misconfigured.

**Fix:**
Check the service status and logs for errors.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new LLM APIs and improved logging features.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see output similar to 'NAME READY STATUS RESTARTS AGE' with your LiteLLM pods listed and their status as 'Running'.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the necessary tools and resources before proceeding with the installation.
